     The Numbers Business Report

Beasts of the Southern Wild (2012)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $12,795,746Details
International Box Office $10,469,386
Worldwide Box Office $23,265,132
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $7,721,968
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $3,882,741
Total $11,604,709
Further financial details...

Synopsis

In a forgotten but defiant bayou community cut off from the rest of the world by a sprawling levee, a six-year-old girl exists on the brink of orphanhood. Buoyed by her childish optimism and extraordinary imagination, she believes that the natural world is in balance with the universe until a fierce storm changes her reality. Desperate to repair the structure of her world in order to save her ailing father and sinking home, this tiny hero must learn to survive unstoppable catastrophes of epic proportions.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$169,702 (1.3% of total gross)
Legs:11.08 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:55.0% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$1,800,000 (worldwide box office is 12.9 times production budget)
Theater counts:4 opening theaters/318 max. theaters, 10.3 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $19,177,179

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: June 27th, 2012 (Limited) by Fox Searchlight
International Releases: August 9th, 2012 (Wide) (Czech Republic)
September 13th, 2012 (Wide) (Australia)
October 12th, 2012 (Wide) (Poland)
October 18th, 2012 (Wide) (Netherlands)
October 19th, 2012 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
Video Release: December 4th, 2012 by Fox Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for thematic material including child imperilment, some disturbing images, language and brief sensuality.
(Rating bulletin 2213, 3/7/2012)
Running Time: 93 minutes
Award nomination: 2013 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Cast, crew, or production detail: Voiceover/Narration
Plot point: Coming of Age, Dysfunctional Family, Environment, Refugee, Single Parent, Terminal Illness, Extreme Weather
Social setting: End of the World
Source material: Novel or Other Work Adapted by Author
Subgenre: Family Drama
Source:Based on Play
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Fantasy
Production/Financing Companies: Fox Searchlight Pictures, Cinereach Films, Court 13, Journeyman Pictures
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Full financial estimates for this film, including domestic and international box office, video sales, video rentals, TV and ancillary revenue are available through our research services. For more information, please contact us at research@the-numbers.com.


  • Summary
  • Box Office
  • Full Financials

Synopsis

In a forgotten but defiant bayou community cut off from the rest of the world by a sprawling levee, a six-year-old girl exists on the brink of orphanhood. Buoyed by her childish optimism and extraordinary imagination, she believes that the natural world is in balance with the universe until a fierce storm changes her reality. Desperate to repair the structure of her world in order to save her ailing father and sinking home, this tiny hero must learn to survive unstoppable catastrophes of epic proportions.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$169,702 (1.3% of total gross)
Legs:11.08 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:55.0% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$1,800,000 (worldwide box office is 12.9 times production budget)
Theater counts:4 opening theaters/318 max. theaters, 10.3 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $19,177,179

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: June 27th, 2012 (Limited) by Fox Searchlight
International Releases: August 9th, 2012 (Wide) (Czech Republic)
September 13th, 2012 (Wide) (Australia)
October 12th, 2012 (Wide) (Poland)
October 18th, 2012 (Wide) (Netherlands)
October 19th, 2012 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
Video Release: December 4th, 2012 by Fox Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for thematic material including child imperilment, some disturbing images, language and brief sensuality.
(Rating bulletin 2213, 3/7/2012)
Running Time: 93 minutes
Award nomination: 2013 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Cast, crew, or production detail: Voiceover/Narration
Plot point: Coming of Age, Dysfunctional Family, Environment, Refugee, Single Parent, Terminal Illness, Extreme Weather
Social setting: End of the World
Source material: Novel or Other Work Adapted by Author
Subgenre: Family Drama
Source:Based on Play
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Fantasy
Production/Financing Companies: Fox Searchlight Pictures, Cinereach Films, Court 13, Journeyman Pictures
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English
